title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Patriots are considering adding Tyreek Hill, Marquise Brown, Jonnu Smith, Bobby Okereke, and Kenneth Murray as free agents.
Tyreek Hill is a potential deep-threat addition, recovering from injury, while Marquise Brown offers similar speed.
Okereke and Murray are linebacker options, with Okereke more proven and likely more expensive than Murray.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led Boston with 24 points, 10 rebounds, and two blocks in a 111-100 win against the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum made 20 points and 14 rebounds but struggled with 3-point shooting, hitting only two of nine attempts.
Luka Garza contributed 15 points in 15 minutes, helping mitigate the absence of injured center Nikola Vucevic.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys began their St. Patrick's Day weekend shows with a performance at MGM Music Hall on March 13.
The concert featured opening acts The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown on stage before the main performance.
Dropkick Murphys' lead singer Ken Casey engaged with the crowd, supported by bandmates Kevin Rheault and Jeff DaRosa.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC debuted in the NWSL with a 1-0 loss to Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium.
The match, marked by physicality, broke attendance records with over 30,000 spectators and tied for most yellow cards in an NWSL game.
Gotham FC coach praised Boston's efforts and noted their potential for future success in the league.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall, kicking off St. Patrick's Day celebrations with a high-energy show.
Ken Casey addressed political themes, criticizing ICE policies and performing 'Citizen I.C.E.' to protest recent incidents.
The band mixed classic hits with new songs, debuting 'Only the Strong' from their upcoming EP 'New England Forever.'

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
He was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11 and supports the Joslin Diabetes Center.
Bennett runs to inspire others living with diabetes and raise awareness for a cure.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter of Springfield holds the record for most Oscar nominations by a Black woman with her fifth nomination.
Carter was the first Black person to win the Oscar for Best Costume Design, winning in 2019 and again in 2023.
Carter is recognized for her work on 'Sinners,' which received a historic 16 nominations at the upcoming Oscars.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if a proposed property-tax override is not approved by voters.
The district requires $18.5 million over three years to maintain current staff and avoid cutting programs.
Brookline's school budget issues stem from rising costs and restricted revenue growth under Proposition 2½.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act would ban the sale of dogs and cats in pet shops in Massachusetts.
The bill proposes allowing pet shops to partner with shelters for animal adoptions instead of selling pets.
The legislation aims to remove housing and insurance barriers to make pet ownership more accessible.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts officials urge water conservation due to ongoing drought, despite February's heavy snowfall.
Central and Northeast Massachusetts are at Level 3 Critical Drought, while other areas face milder drought levels.
Snowfall provided limited relief; persistent precipitation deficits and low groundwater levels continue to strain water resources.
